items.title: Access
items.text: Access to the servers is done by SSH from the login machine. This guide describes how to log in using SSH and how to set up passwordless SSH login.

items.title: Your first login
items.text:
When getting access to a Linux server, you will be given a set of credentials and a machine name or an IP address for your Linux server. After you have gotten your login credentials, open the command prompt window (Win+R, type cmd.exe and hit Enter) on the login machine and type the following:

IPMachine name 
ssh user@<linux server IP>

ssh user@<machine-name>

You will be prompted to enter your password. Verify that you can log in using the password provided.

items.title: Setting up your SSH config file
items.text:
Create the .ssh folder if it does not already exist by typing the following into the command prompt window:

mkdir "%USERPROFILE%\.ssh"
Create or verify that an SSH config file exists by running:

type nul >> "%USERPROFILE%\.ssh\config"

Open the SSH config file in Notepad:

Notepad.exe "%USERPROFILE%\.ssh\config"

Add your SSH configuration to the SSH config file and save it. You may choose the alias as you'd like. From now on, you will log in to the machine using ssh <alias>, so I like to give it short and descriptive aliases such as linux or gpu.

SSH config file
Host <alias>
    HostName <IP or machine name>
    User <username>

Tip

Verify that you can log in using ssh <alias> before you move on. You still need to provide your password.

items.title: Get started
items.text:
This is the documentation for Linux and GPU servers at Helsedatasenteret at St. Olavs hospital (HDS). The Linux CPU and GPU servers are an offer to researchers that:

  • have more complex needs
  • require additional compute
  • require a Linux environment

This documentation describes how the servers are set up and the tools that are pre-installed.

items.title: Quickstart
items.text:
PythonR

For Python development, start by getting access, setting up Visual Studio Code, and setting up a Python environment.

For R development, start by getting access and setting up R server.

items.title: Commands
items.text:
• zensical new - Create a new project
  • zensical serve - Start local web server
  • zensical build - Build your site

greet("Python")

Code can also be highlighted inline: print("Hello, Python!").

Go to documentation

\[ \cos x=\sum_{k=0}^{\infty}\frac{(-1)^k}{(2k)!}x^{2k} \]

Needs configuration

Note that MathJax is included via a script tag on this page and is not configured in the generated default configuration to avoid including it in a pages that do not need it. See the documentation for details on how to configure it on all your pages if they are more Maths-heavy than these simple starter pages.
